---
title: "ProSIFT Module 04b: QC Report Assembly"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 3
    theme: default
---

**Last Updated:** 2026-03-27
**Module Status:** Stub -- not yet designed
**Pipeline Position:** [Module 04: Imputation] --> **Module 04b: QC Report Assembly** --> [Module 05: Differential Abundance]

---

# 1. Purpose and Scope

This is a lightweight assembly process that compiles diagnostic outputs from Modules 02-04 into a single consolidated HTML QC report. It serves as the formal checkpoint between the data preparation stages (QC, normalization, imputation) and the analytical stages (differential abundance, enrichment).

The report is designed to be reviewed by the analyst before differential abundance proceeds. It does not modify any data -- it only collects and renders existing outputs.

**[FOLLOW-UP NEEDED]** Full design pending. Report sections, plot layout, and pass/warn/fail flag thresholds need to be defined once Modules 02-04 are designed.

---

# 2. Inputs and Outputs

## 2.1 Inputs

- Pre-normalization QC diagnostics (from Module 02)
- Post-normalization diagnostics (from Module 03)
- CV summary table and imputation diagnostics (from Module 04)
- params.yml (for threshold values used in flag evaluation)

## 2.2 Outputs

- Consolidated QC report (HTML, interactive Plotly plots)
- QC flag summary table (Parquet + CSV): per-sample and per-protein flags aggregated across Modules 02-04

---

# 3. Sub-Workflow Diagram

*[To be designed.]*

---

# 4. Process Specifications

*[To be designed. Will cover:]*

- Report section layout (pre-norm QC, normalization diagnostics, imputation summary, CV distributions)
- QC flag schema: per-sample flags (outlier, low detection rate) and per-protein flags (high CV, high missingness)
- Pass/warn/fail thresholds for each flag type

---

# 5. Design Decisions

### 2026-03-27 -- QC Report Assembly as Separate Process

**Decision:** QC Report Assembly is a distinct Nextflow process (Module 04b) rather than a section of Module 04 (Imputation) or Module 05 (Differential Abundance).

**Rationale:** The report draws on outputs from three upstream modules (02, 03, 04). Making it a section of any one of them would create an awkward dependency chain or require passing diagnostic outputs forward for no analytical reason. A dedicated assembly process with explicit inputs from all three upstream modules is cleaner and makes the checkpoint intent explicit.

**Alternatives considered:** Include report assembly in Module 04 as a final step -- rejected because it would require Module 04 to depend on Module 02 and 03 diagnostic outputs, complicating the DAG. Include it in Module 05 as a pre-analysis check -- rejected because it would couple the checkpoint to the analytical module.

---

# 6. Parameters

*[To be defined when Modules 02-04 are designed. Will include flag thresholds referenced during report assembly.]*

---

# 7. Known Limitations and Edge Cases

- The QC report is advisory -- it documents data quality and flags potential issues but does not automatically halt the pipeline.
- Flag thresholds are heuristic; appropriate values may differ between tissue types, sample preparation methods, or abundance dynamic ranges.

---

# 8. Testing Notes

*[To be populated during implementation.]*

---

# 9. Change History

### 2026-03-27 -- Created

**Change:** New module stub created as part of the pipeline architecture revision (2026-03-27 session).
**Reason:** QC Report Assembly identified as a dedicated checkpoint process between the data preparation stages (Modules 02-04) and the analytical stages (Modules 05+). Using "04b" numbering to indicate it follows Module 04 but precedes Module 05 without disrupting the module numbering of analytical stages.
**Affected processes:** N/A (stub only).
